entities.name: LAPSUS_LS
entities.description: A physical landslide model that models slope stability at the catchment scale by combining hydrological and Limit Equilibrium Method models

entities.name: HYDROLOGICAL MODEL
entities.description: A model used within LAPSUS_LS to account for water movement

entities.name: LIMIT EQUILIBRIUM METHOD
entities.description: A model used within LAPSUS_LS to calculate the factor of safety for slope stability

entities.name: SLOPE STABILITY
entities.description: The resistance of a slope to landslide failure

entities.name: CATCHMENT LEVEL
entities.description: The spatial scale at which landslide models are applied

entities.name: FACTOR OF SAFETY
entities.description: A calculated value used to determine if a slope is stable or unstable

entities.name: COFFEE
entities.description: Coffea arabica, tested as a monoculture for its effect on slope stability

entities.name: ERYTHRINA
entities.description: Erythrina poeppigiana, deep-rooting trees tested in mixed plantation for slope stability

entities.name: COSTA RICA
entities.description: The region from which soil and root data were obtained for simulations

entities.name: ROOT REINFORCEMENT
entities.description: The mechanical stabilization of soil provided by plant roots

entities.name: SOIL BULK DENSITY
entities.description: The mass of soil per unit volume, used as a parameter in LAPSUS_LS

entities.name: TRANSMISSIVITY
entities.description: The rate at which water is transmitted through soil

entities.name: INTERNAL FRICTION ANGLE
entities.description: A soil property representing the shear strength of the soil

entities.name: DEPTH OF SHEAR PLANE
entities.description: The depth at which the failure surface occurs in a landslide

entities.name: BIOMASS SURCHARGE EFFECT
entities.description: The additional load placed on a slope by the weight of vegetation biomass

entities.name: ADDITIONAL COHESION
entities.description: The increase in soil cohesion provided by the presence of roots
